title: Method and system for generating forestry data
abstract: Disclosed is a method for generating forestry data of a forest (204) having a plurality of trees (304, 306, 308, 310). The method comprises defining a route (202) within the forest (204) for repetitively collecting data as data points using at least one LiDAR (106, 302), repetitively assigning a unique swipe identifier to the data points, and repetitively storing the data points and the swipe identifier to a point cloud database, processing the point cloud database for creating a three-dimensional model of the forest, processing the three-dimensional model to identify a number of trees, assigning and storing a unique tree identifier for each tree in a tree database, processing the three-dimensional model and the assigned tree identifiers for segmenting data points having a given tree identifier for a trunk of the tree, generating a representation of a trunk of the tree, and calculating parameters for the tree from the representation to form the forestry data.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for generating and processing a three-dimensional model of a forest (204) for gathering forestry data of the forest (204) having a plurality of trees (304, 306, 308, 310), the method comprising : (i) defining a route (202) within the forest (204) for collecting data, wherein a boundary of the forest area (204) for which the data is required is defined and the route (202) has a sufficient number of crossings (206) to cover each of the plurality of trees within the route; and recording all the trees and the data associated with the trees; (ii) collecting data as data points using at least one LiDAR (106, 302) automatically maneuvering along the entire defined route (202), wherein at least one device (102) is used for maneuvering the at least one LiDAR automatically and wherein geographical co-ordinates associated with a position of the LiDAR changes and the data is collected during a single swipe of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302); (iii) assigning a unique swipe identifier to the data points collected during the single swipe of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302), storing the data points and the swipe identifier to a point cloud database, the data corresponding to each of the swipe identifier is processed individually and partitioning the data collected as the point cloud into a plurality of cubical sections; (iv) repeating steps (ii) and (iii); (v) capturing an image of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) and associating the image to the point cloud corresponding to the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310); (vi) processing the point cloud database using a Simultaneous Localization and Mapping algorithm for creating a three-dimensional model of the forest (204); (vii) processing the three-dimensional model of the forest to identify a number of trees (304, 306, 308, 310), assigning a unique tree identifier 

 to each tree of the number of trees (304, 306, 308, 310), and storing the tree identifier to a tree database; (viii) processing the three-dimensional model of the forest (204) and the assigned tree identifiers, for segmenting data points having a given tree identifier as belonging to a trunk of the tree (304, 306, 308, 310); (ix) generating a representation (616) of a trunk of at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310), by: (a) using the data associated with said tree (304, 306, 308, 310), based on the tree identifier; (b) selecting a data set having a given swipe identifier, wherein each swipe creates the data points in a shape of an arc (404, 406, 408, 410) around the trunk of the tree (304, 306, 308, 310), normalizing the data points in the shape of an arc (404, 406, 408, 410) and fitting into a circle, and a diameter of the circle corresponds to a diameter of the at least one tree trunk; (c) repeating step (b) to generate a plurality of circles corresponding to a plurality of portions of the at least one tree trunk; and (d) generating a representation of said tree trunk using the plurality of circles; and (x) calculating parameters for the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) from the representation of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) to form the forestry data. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein the swipe identifier comprises: - a location of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302); - an inclination of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302) with respect to ground; and - a timestamp of the data collection. 3. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising : 

 - segmenting the representation of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) into at least one of the tree trunk, branches, or leaves; - calculating parameters for at least one of the tree trunk, branches, or leaves of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310); - determining a species of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) from the parameters; and - storing the representation and the species of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) in the tree database. 4. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one device (102) is an autonomous vehicle selected from an unmanned aerial vehicle, a drone, an autonomous ground vehicle, a ground robot and a combination of at least two same or different types of autonomous vehicles. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the Simultaneous Localization and Mapping is further used for calculating the location of the autonomous vehicle maneuvering through the defined route for automated data collection. 6. A method according to claim 5, wherein the method further comprises obtaining a map of the maneuvering route of the autonomous vehicle. 7. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a combination of data collected by the IMU, GPS, LiDAR and camera is used for calculating trajectory of the maneuvering defined route. 8. A system for generating and processing a three-dimensional model of a forest (204) for gathering forestry data of the forest (204) having a plurality of trees (304, 306, 308, 310) configured to perform the method of any of claims 1-3, the system (100) comprising: - at least one device (102) comprising a data processing arrangement (104), at least one automatically maneuverable LiDAR (106, 302) and a 

 camera configured to capturing an image of the at least one tree, wherein the device (102) is configured to: (i) collect data as data points using at least one LiDAR (106, 302) automatically maneuvered along a route (202), wherein geographical co-ordinates associated with a position of the LiDAR changes and the data is collected during a single swipe of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302); (ii) assign a unique swipe identifier to the data points collected during the single swipe of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302), storing the data points and the swipe identifier to a point cloud database; and (iii) repeat steps (i) and (ii); - a server arrangement (108) communicatively coupled to the data processing arrangement (104) of the device (102), wherein the server arrangement (108) is configured to: - define the route (202) within the forest (204) for collecting data, wherein a boundary of the forest area (204) for which the data is required is defined and the route (202) has a sufficient number of crossings (206) to cover each of the plurality of trees within the route and recording all the trees and the data associated with the trees; - process the point cloud database using Simultaneous Localization and Mapping algorithm for creating a three-dimensional model of the forest (204); - associate the image to the point cloud corresponding to the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310); - process the three-dimensional model of the forest (204) to identify a number of trees (304, 306, 308, 310), assigning a unique tree identifier to each tree of the number of trees (304, 306, 308, 310), and storing the tree identifier to a tree database; 

 - process the three-dimensional model of the forest (204) and the assigned tree identifiers, for segmenting data points having a given tree identifier as belonging to a trunk of the tree (304, 306, 308, 310); - generate a representation of a trunk of at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310), by: (a) using the data associated with said tree (304, 306, 308, 310), based on the tree identifier; (b) selecting a data set having a given swipe, wherein each swipe creates the data points in a shape of an arc (404, 406, 408, 410) around the trunk of the tree (304, 306, 308, 310), normalizing the data points in a shape of an arc (404, 406, 408, 410) and fitting into a circle and a diameter of the circle corresponds to a diameter of the at least one tree trunk; (c) repeating step (b) to generate a plurality of circles (504, 506, 508) corresponding to a plurality of portions of the at least one tree trunk; and (d) generating a representation of said tree trunk using the plurality of circles (504, 506, 508); - calculate parameters for the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) from the representation of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) to form the forestry data; and - partition the data collected as the point cloud into a plurality of cubical sections; and a database arrangement (110) communicatively coupled to each of the device (102) and the server arrangement (108), wherein the database arrangement (110) comprises the point cloud database and the tree database. 9. A system according to claim 8, wherein the LiDAR (106, 302) is implemented as one of: a rotating LiDAR, a solid-state LiDAR. 10. A system according to claim 9, wherein the device (102) further comprises a location determination unit, an inertial measurement unit (IMU) and a clock; and wherein the swipe identifier comprises: - a location of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302) determined using the location determination unit; - an inclination of the at least one LiDAR (106, 302) with respect to ground determined using the inertial measurement unit; and - a timestamp of the data collection determined using the clock. 11. A system according to claim 8, wherein the camera is implemented as: a digital camera, or a hyperspectral camera. 12. A system according to any one of the claims 8-11, wherein the server arrangement (108) is further configured to: - segment the representation of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) into at least one of the tree trunk, branches, or leaves; - calculate parameters for at least one of the tree trunk, branches, or leaves of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310); - determine a species of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) from the parameters; and - store the representation and the species of the at least one tree (304, 306, 308, 310) in the tree database. 13. A system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one device (102) is an autonomous vehicle selected from an unmanned aerial vehicle, a drone, an autonomous ground vehicle, a ground robot and a combination of at least two same or different types of autonomous vehicles.
